meaning: n.蒸发(物),散发,[生]蒸腾作用,[物]流逸
examples: Air temperature and vapor pressure deficit were the key factors affecting transpiration.
But transpiration is harder to deal with than perspiration.
Most of the remaining 10% is released by plants through transpiration.
This bare, sandy earth then becomes an effective rain trap - with no vegetation, water cannot be lost through transpiration (the evaporation of water from plants).
